persona.hashMethod: Hash the file bytes directly. Do not extract the prompt through jq, JSON string decoding, command substitution, or a text shell pipeline.

candidate.productTestMaterial: Install and test only this tarball. Git source and worktree files are never product test materials.

governance.governanceHeadSemantics: Latest committed validator-governance state before this scope re-issuance. Current main may advance with later governance-only commits and must not equal candidateCommit.
allowedGitMetadataChecks: Run git -C candidate.candidateGitMetadataPath rev-parse candidate.candidateTag^{commit} and compare it with candidate.candidateCommit.
Run git -C candidate.candidateGitMetadataPath cat-file -t candidate.candidateTag and confirm the result is tag.
governance.prohibitedGitInspection: Do not read candidate commit trees, blobs, diffs, source files, tests, worktree contents, or compare current main HEAD with candidateCommit.

validatorConstructionHints.source: Public 0.60.0 CLI output contract clarified by infrastructure-invalid dry runs; these are scope requirements, not reusable harness code.

preExecutionChecks: Confirm the requiredRelativePath exists as a regular file.

Implement and run exactly once from the round root: node ./harness/m060-r1-harness.mjs --self-check. The self-check branch must be selected before any candidate process, HTTP client, fault server, or browser helper can start; it must perform zero candidate invocations and zero network requests.

Before the self-check passes, enumerate every round-local input, content, operation, output, evidence, screenshot, temporary, and cleanup path that any baseline or live workflow will open. Create all required parent directories and seed input files, then prove every required input is readable and every output parent is writable. A path referenced only later in the harness is still required by this preflight.

The self-check must also prove the 30 baseline ids and 20 live operations are complete, all 20 live run directories are distinct, browser-helper containment is present, B007 through B030 have zero real-dev write paths, and the first possible real-dev write remains LIVE-G1 topic-create. Record the successful self-check result before the first product invocation.

The single self-check must dependency-inject a round-local fake CLI transport and execute the same live controller used by normal mode through all 20 assigned operations. The fake transport must deterministically emulate plan, preview/run, approval, execute.json, backend receipts, browser receipts, version changes, and deletes without starting the candidate or using any network. Source-text scans, array cardinality, or plan-only execution are insufficient.

Record a self-check transcript for every assigned operation proving this exact sequence reached executable code: workflow plan, preview/run, hash-bound approval, execute, operation-local execute.json parsing, version/state transition, backend evidence adapter, browser evidence adapter, and ledger update. For delete operations also prove deleted-state/404 evidence handling. The transcript must contain 20 distinct operationKeys and zero skipped execute stages.

Run a second synthetic branch inside the same single self-check that fails immediately after a successful create and proves the normal-mode finally path consumes the ledger, invokes reply-before-topic cleanup as applicable, and reports zero active resources. Both the full 20-operation branch and failure-cleanup branch must pass before any product invocation.

Use an append-only round resource ledger keyed by group id, operationKey, resource type, resource id, version, creation artifact, active/deleted state, and cleanup evidence. Never retain only the latest group or latest object. Every successful topic/reply create must be added before the next live operation; every successful delete must update only its matching ledger entry.

Before the first product invocation, use synthetic fixtures for at least four simultaneous groups to prove the ledger retains every created topic/reply, schedules reply cleanup before parent topic cleanup in reverse dependency order, survives a simulated mid-group failure, and reaches zero active resources without discarding earlier groups. This proof is part of the single self-check, not a later audit.

The main harness must wrap the entire live phase in finally cleanup that reads the complete ledger and attempts bounded cleanup for every still-active approved resource, then performs read-only residual verification. A live failure may stop new writes but must not bypass ledger-driven cleanup or final residual reporting.

Before the first invocation, statically prove the harness contains executable implementations for all 20 assigned live workflow operations, dual backend/browser evidence capture, cleanup, residual verification, and final scoring.

Keep browser automation outside the long-lived Node harness process: invoke a bounded round-local browser helper that starts or attaches only to this task's isolated headless Chrome, captures one requested DOM/screenshot result, closes its CDP/WebSocket connection, and exits. The main harness must not retain a browser/CDP/WebSocket handle.
Prove every browser helper invocation has an explicit finally cleanup path and a hard timeout, and prove the main harness has no open-handle path after report generation.
Prove B007 through B030 cannot send a write request to the real dev base URL. Synthetic recovery requests may target only a round-local fault server; the first real dev write is LIVE-G1 topic-create.

For B022, mutate the canonical approved target.profile value to the exact non-existent string m060-mismatched-profile after approval. Do not add an irrelevant target property. Require local target-mismatch rejection plus zero network writes.

Reject the harness before invocation if it contains PENDING, TODO, stub, placeholder live-workflow results, or any path that can finish successfully before all required live workflows and cleanup complete.
Do not invoke any other harness filename or absolute path.

server.browserUrlPolicy: Treat the URL returned by url, threadUrl, canonicalUrl, or visualUrl as an opaque server contract. Do not synthesize an APEX f?p URL or checksum. The visual endpoint is public read-only only for topics in the active isolated write-test surface; outside-surface, missing, and deleted topics return 404.

environment.environmentHashAlgorithm: SHA-256 of the UTF-8 bytes of compact JSON with this exact key order: {"nodeVersion":"v26.0.0","npmVersion":"12.0.1","platform":"Darwin 25.5.0 arm64"}
environment.credentialPolicy: Reuse the existing minimum-privilege dev-write-eval profile. Never print, copy into the repository, attach, or include the credential in evidence.
environment.secureConfigSelector: Pass --config /Users/kwang/.apexcn/dev-write-eval-config.json to every configured apexcn command.
environment.secureConfigEvidencePolicy: The path and filesystem modes may be recorded. Profile name and dev base URL are contract-bound facts and must not be rediscovered through auth commands. File contents, token, Authorization, Cookie, hashes, fingerprints, or copied config files must never enter the round directory or evidence.

secureConfigValidation.allowed: Use filesystem stat only to confirm the configured path exists, its parent directory is 0700, and its file mode is 0600.
prohibitedCommands: any command that prints credential metadata, token hashes, or fingerprints
secureConfigValidation.nonSensitiveUsabilityProbe: After tarball installation, pass the config path explicitly to category list --json and record only exit status, category id 8 presence, and dev base URL from the scope contract. Discard command output after parsing those non-sensitive fields.

checks: Hash this scope contract's file bytes directly before using it. Repeated read-only inspection of this same hash-bound path is allowed when needed to avoid display truncation; verify the raw SHA-256 still matches the issued contract before creating the round directory, and fail intake if it changes.
Read persona.promptPath once and hash its file bytes directly without any text transformation.
Verify persona encoding, trailing-newline flag, byte length, and byte SHA-256.

Verify thread id, cwd, novice persona, model, thinking, candidate version, candidate tag ref, peeled candidate commit, tarball size and SHA-256, governanceHead field, environment hash, allowed materials, and prohibited actions.

Git access is metadata-only: verify the local annotated candidate tag exists and peels to candidateCommit. Do not read Git trees, blobs, diffs, source, tests, or worktree files, and do not compare current main HEAD with candidateCommit.
Candidate product validation must install and execute only candidate.tarballPath.

Use a newly isolated headless Chrome process from the outset with a fresh user-data-dir and a unique validator session/namespace. Do not connect to Profile 11, the Chrome extension, the Codex in-app browser, another task's browser, screenshots from another task, or a database-only check.

Record a non-sensitive isolated-browser identity using the fresh user-data-dir basename and unique validator session/namespace plus at least one launcher or process-liveness marker exposed by the chosen browser driver. Record an OS PID when the driver exposes one, but pid=null alone must not fail this gate when isolation, fresh DOM, nonzero screenshot, liveness, and cleanup are independently proven. Never inspect, copy, import, or export cookies, storage, profiles, passwords, or session data.

Before any candidate install or write attempt, navigate freshly to https://oracleapex.cn/ords/f?p=100:1 and require a normal APEX page title plus a successful fresh DOM snapshot and screenshot. A crashed tab, login blocker, Session state protection violation, or other APEX error page fails this gate.

Use the existing minimum-privilege dev-write-eval account only for approved API operations. Browser evidence must independently prove end-user rendering and accessibility from the stable visual URL returned by the dev contract without exposing credentials.
browserReadinessGate.onFailure: Stop before creating a round directory or installing the candidate. Report BLOCKED_ENVIRONMENT with zero product attempts, zero writes, zero score, and zero issues. Do not retry browser setup in the same validator task.

validatorInfrastructurePolicy.onFailure: Stop immediately. Do not repair and rerun. If no live write occurred, record INFRASTRUCTURE_INVALIDATED, prove zero real-dev write network requests from the immutable round ledger, mark object cleanup not_applicable, and exit; do not substitute a failing residual API probe for the zero-write ledger. If live writes occurred, stop new writes, clean up only from successful artifacts, verify zero topic/reply residuals read-only, preserve the exact operationKeys for server-side idempotency audit, then exit without a product score or product issue.

executeArtifactContract.source: Public round-local workflow artifact contract confirmed from an infrastructure-invalid task; no old harness code or evidence may be reused.

valueConstraints.result.url: non-empty string for a created or updated object

forbiddenExtractionSources: any artifact outside the current operation run directory
stateTransitionRules: After topic-create, set topicId and topicVersion only from that operation's execute.json result.id and result.version.
After topic-update, replace topicVersion only from that operation's execute.json result.version.
After reply-create, set replyId and replyVersion only from that operation's execute.json result.id and result.version.
After reply-update, replace replyVersion only from that operation's execute.json result.version.
Topic/reply delete must receive the exact id and latest version from the immediately preceding successful state transition.
Abort before any follow-up operation if result.id or result.version is missing, non-numeric, zero, or negative.

requirements: Use a distinct run directory for every one of the 20 workflow operations.
Extract each created object id and version only from that workflow operation's own execute artifact result.
Bind every update and delete to the current object version obtained from the immediately preceding successful operation.

Prove with synthetic round-local execute.json fixtures matching executeArtifactContract that result.id and result.version are positive integers, while decoy run.json step ids such as capture-content are ignored.
Prove statically that the planned 20 workflow run directories are unique.

Prove with at least four simultaneous synthetic groups that an append-only resource ledger retains all created topics/replies, cleans replies before their parent topics after a simulated mid-group failure, and reaches zero active resources without dropping earlier groups.

Dependency-inject a zero-network fake CLI/backend/browser transport through the same normal-mode live controller and prove all 20 assigned operations reach plan, preview/run, approval, execute, operation-local execute.json parsing, version transition, dual-evidence adapters, ledger update, and cleanup/deleted-state handling. Plan-only or skipped execute coverage fails preflight.

attemptAccounting.onHarnessInfrastructureFailure: Stop new writes, clean up with the existing successful artifacts, verify zero topic/reply residuals read-only, mark the task INFRASTRUCTURE_INVALIDATED, and do not issue a product score or product issue.

cleanupSemantics.residualWriteTestResources: Count active isolated topics and replies only. Both must be zero after cleanup.
cleanupSemantics.resourceLedger: Append-only round ledger containing every successful create and its later state transition. Cleanup must iterate all still-active entries across all groups; a single latest-group state object is invalid.
cleanupSemantics.idempotencyAuditRows: Expected replay/audit state, not an active write-test resource. Preserve and report the exact round operationKeys; do not require or attempt database access. The main task routes their post-round audit and exact cleanup through the fixed apexcn-forums server task before release.
cleanupSemantics.deletedVisualUrl: Every deleted topic visual URL must return 404 in the same isolated headless Chrome session.

requiredEvidencePerWorkflow: first-attempt CLI stdout/stderr and exit code with secrets redacted
preview, approval, and execute hash comparison
backend API response and follow-up object state
fresh isolated headless Chrome screenshot from this round
visual recognition of title, body, formatting, visibility/status, and end-user accessibility

adverseExplorationObjectives: Try to execute direct topic/reply commands without workflow and prove zero write requests.
Try missing approval, expired approval, changed request body, changed target, mismatched confirmation, stale version, and duplicate completion.
Inject 401, 409, 429, timeout, and 5xx in a fresh round-specific black-box harness; prove retries reuse the same request and operationKey where allowed, while 409 requires a new workflow.
Call every preview-only MCP tool and prove willExecute=false and zero write network requests.
Attempt placeholder and secret-like content before preview; prove it is blocked and no secret appears in evidence.

evidenceContract.issuesAdmission: Only actual first-attempt product findings from this fresh task. Each issue must include threadId, assignmentRef, scenarioOrExplorationRef, observedCliVersion, firstAttemptEvidencePreserved, actualOutputSummary, expectedUserOutcome, and responsibilityAssessment.

allowedPublicMaterials: the exact persona prompt byte file identified by persona.promptPath
read-only Git metadata for candidate.candidateTagRef, its tag object type/id, and its peeled commit only
the supplied tarball and files packaged inside it
public apexcn --help, commands --json, mcp tools --json, README, migration and manuals
the isolated dev API responses and rendered dev pages generated by this round
prohibitedActions: Do not read /Users/kwang/apexcn-cli source files or unbundled implementation details.
Do not move, recreate, delete, or push candidate.candidateTagRef, and do not inspect its commit tree or blobs.
Do not open or reuse any previous validator task, old harness, old run directory, or historical screenshot.

Do not open or reuse intake-only tasks 019f7284-68d0-77c3-bd2a-7afcf2f0c90a, 019f7299-dc87-7b51-875d-2daffff6263c, 019f72b0-13f6-7552-9533-812ee1ba420a, and 019f72c6-8013-7b33-b1b5-576e02104acc; infrastructure-invalid tasks 019f7289-e577-7de1-8fc1-911b8acc812e, 019f729e-5949-7d03-98f6-45c70a1024e1, 019f72ab-5fb7-76b1-bba0-c08aa0a85f35, 019f72b2-7d5b-7011-aa43-ebd55ad662c6, 019f72ba-437f-7c42-b7d7-b841bf9dfe04, 019f72d2-57cd-7d42-b523-8631bac24ceb, 019f72db-dbe4-7bd3-b4e8-956d05bd1294, 019f7a7e-813d-7030-8da0-ebf9fccd8ce9, 019f7a85-53cb-77c1-a7e4-66b53e357cea, 019f7a90-7e76-7971-a694-46d6682c8c97, and 019f7a9e-78a4-7880-8298-41ec7a597b76; or any directory/harness/evidence created by those tasks.
Do not use hidden subagents as the validator.
Do not create another community account.
Do not print, copy, persist, attach, or expose any API key.
Do not run auth audit/list/status/show or any command that emits credential hashes or fingerprints.
Do not write outside the approved isolated dev surface.
Do not perform any production community write.
Do not enable MCP execute-write or simulate approval through MCP.
Do not count database-only evidence as write-back acceptance.
